LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT KONSTANTIN RADINOVICH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ISSUES IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY BECAUSE PARAGRAPH 48 OF THE COMPLAINT WHICH IS INCORPORATED IN REFERENCE TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION STATES THAT THE SUBJECT INCIDENT, MR. RADINOVICH FAILED TO KEEP A PROPER LOOK OUT AND LOOK TO HIS RIGHT AND WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING THE COLLISSION. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE FOR COMPARATIVE NEGLIGENCE IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS A DISPUTED ISSUE AS WHAT PERCENT MR. HALEY IS AT FAULT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =(302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-29-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of The Issues
Off Calendar Apr-21-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHELLE WEISS OF THE BAY AREA BICYCLE LAW, TEL. NO. 1-415-466-8717 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK O'BRIEN AND ATTY. NICHOLAS BURKE OF THE O'BRIEN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-707-525-8999 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT KONSTANTIN RADINOVICH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of The Issues is denied in its entirety because paragraph 48 of the complaint which is incorporated in reference to the negligence cause of action states that the subject incident, Mr. Radinovich failed to keep a proper look out and look to his right and was a substantial factor in causing the collission. The court finds that there is a substantial if not overwhelming contributory evidence and Mr. Radinovich has failed to meet his initial summary Judgment burden of showing that he kept a proper look out. Summary Adjudication on the Second cause of action is Denied. Summary Adjudication on the affirmative defense for comparative negligence is denied because there is a disputed issue as what percent Mr. Haley is at fault. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Bifurcate Trial
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, and Raiser-CA, LLC's motion to bifurcate trial is denied without prejudice to being renewed before the trial judge. The trial judge, not a law and motion judge, is in the best position to determine whether bifurcation will serve judicial economy. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
